title: QUIC Local Interop-Runner Execution-Report Helper Verification
verification_method: Local execution, artifact inspection, and report review.
preconditions: Docker is available on the local machine.
Python 3 is available for the external runner checkout.
The local runner checkout exists at `C:\src\quic-interop\quic-interop-runner`.
The supported INT harness slices remain available through the existing local harness path.
scope: Verify that the local helper can build the harness image, invoke the external runner through image replacement, and capture runner JSON, Markdown, stdout/stderr, and log artifacts under a repo-local execution-report tree without modifying the runner repository.
procedure: Run the helper against the local runner checkout with the default both-role replacement slot and the supported testcase subset.

Confirm the helper creates a timestamped artifact root under `artifacts/interop-runner/` and that it contains the runner JSON report, the captured Markdown or console table, the log directory tree, and the harness image build output.
Inspect the captured output to verify unsupported testcases still map to the runner's honest unsupported result and build failures remain build failures rather than pass results.
If the tree is temporarily mid-edit and the first run fails for an unrelated build reason, wait briefly and rerun once to confirm the helper still behaves honestly after the transient issue clears.
expected_result: The helper produces repo-local execution-report artifacts for a local runner run without requiring any change to the runner repository, and unsupported or build-blocked cases are not misreported as successful runs.
status_summary: Planned; this verification slice records the local-only execution-report helper proof contract.

supplemental_sections.heading: Deferred Coverage
supplemental_sections.content: No runner repository registry change, no new testcase enablement, no qlog-format changes, and no transport/runtime behavior changes are in scope for this verification slice.
